questions.q: How many years was Rama's forest exile (vanavasa) to last?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi demanded that Rama be banished to the forest for fourteen years.

questions.q: Whose word did Rama uphold by accepting the exile?
questions.explanation: Rama accepted exile to keep his father Dasharatha's promise to Kaikeyi.

questions.q: Who demanded that Rama be sent into exile?
questions.explanation: Queen Kaikeyi used her two boons to demand Rama's exile and the throne for Bharata.

questions.q: Which wife of Rama insisted on accompanying him into the forest?
questions.explanation: Sita refused to remain in the palace and chose to share Rama's hardship in the forest.

questions.q: Which brother chose to accompany Rama during the vanavasa?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana left Ayodhya to serve and protect Rama and Sita throughout the exile.

questions.q: From which city was Rama exiled?
questions.explanation: Rama was the prince of Ayodhya, the capital of the Kosala kingdom, from which he was exiled.

questions.q: Who was to be crowned king of Ayodhya instead of Rama?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi's second boon demanded that her son Bharata be installed as king.

questions.q: What central virtue is the vanavasa said to test in Rama?
questions.explanation: Rama's acceptance of exile is held up as the supreme test of dharma at the heart of the epic.

questions.q: How did Rama react when told he must go into exile?
options: He raised an army to fight
He accepted it calmly without complaint
questions.explanation: Rama accepted the banishment without complaint, treating his father's word as binding.

questions.q: Into what kind of place was Rama banished during the vanavasa?
questions.explanation: The word 'vanavasa' literally means 'dwelling in the forest,' the place of Rama's banishment.

questions.q: What two boons did Kaikeyi invoke to bring about the vanavasa?
options: Wealth for Bharata and a palace for herself
The throne for Bharata and fourteen years' exile for Rama
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi reminded Dasharatha of two old boons, demanding Bharata's coronation and Rama's fourteen-year exile.

questions.q: Who poisoned Kaikeyi's mind against Rama before she made her demands?
questions.explanation: Manthara, Kaikeyi's hunchbacked servant, persuaded her to claim the boons against Rama.

questions.q: How did Dasharatha respond emotionally to being forced to exile Rama?
options: He was overcome with grief and despair
questions.explanation: Dasharatha was shattered with grief, bound by his word yet heartbroken at losing Rama.

questions.q: What clothing did Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana put on to mark their exile?
questions.explanation: As ascetics in exile, they exchanged royal garments for bark cloth and deerskin.

questions.q: Who was the charioteer that drove Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana out of Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Sumantra, Dasharatha's trusted charioteer and minister, drove them from the city.

questions.q: Which friendly tribal king ferried Rama across the Ganga during the exile?
questions.explanation: Guha, chief of the Nishada hunters and Rama's friend, helped them cross the Ganga at Shringaverapura.

questions.q: At whose hermitage did Rama and Sita stay early in the exile, receiving guidance on where to dwell?
questions.explanation: Sage Bharadvaja at Prayaga welcomed them and directed them toward Chitrakuta to settle.

questions.q: On which hill did Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana first settle for a period during the exile?
questions.explanation: They built a hermitage and lived for a time on Chitrakuta hill early in the exile.

questions.q: Why did Bharata come to the forest to meet Rama during the exile?
options: To beg Rama to return and rule Ayodhya
To join Rama permanently in exile
To inform Rama of a foreign invasion
questions.explanation: Bharata, refusing the throne, traveled to Chitrakuta to plead with Rama to return as king.

questions.q: What did Bharata take back to Ayodhya to rule on Rama's behalf?
questions.explanation: Bharata placed Rama's sandals on the throne and ruled as a regent in his name.

questions.q: Which event, caused by the exile, occurred in Ayodhya soon after Rama left?
options: The death of Dasharatha from grief
questions.explanation: Dasharatha died of heartbreak shortly after Rama departed, unable to bear the separation.

questions.q: In which of the Ramayana's kandas (books) does the exile and the events leading to it primarily take place?
questions.explanation: The Ayodhya Kanda narrates Kaikeyi's demand and the beginning of Rama's exile.

questions.q: From which earlier event did Dasharatha owe Kaikeyi the two boons she used?
options: Her aid to him in a war between gods and demons
A bet he lost to her
A vow he made at Rama's birth
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi had once saved Dasharatha on the battlefield against the asuras, earning the two boons he granted her.

questions.q: Near which river was the village of the Nishada chief Guha, where Rama spent a night before crossing?
questions.explanation: Guha's town of Shringaverapura lay on the bank of the Ganga, where Rama rested before crossing.

questions.q: Which great sage of the Dandaka forest gave Rama celestial weapons during the exile?
questions.explanation: Sage Agastya gifted Rama divine weapons, including the bow of Vishnu, during his stay in the Dandaka forest.

questions.q: After Chitrakuta, in which forest region did Rama spend much of his exile among the ascetics?
questions.explanation: Rama moved into the vast Dandaka forest, visiting the hermitages of many sages there.

questions.q: On the advice of which sage did Rama finally settle at Panchavati during the exile?
questions.explanation: Sage Agastya advised Rama to build his hermitage at Panchavati on the Godavari river.

questions.q: At whose hermitage did Sita receive hospitality and counsel from the sage's devoted wife Anasuya during the exile?
questions.explanation: At Sage Atri's hermitage, his wife Anasuya welcomed Sita and gave her gifts and advice on wifely devotion.

questions.q: Whose grief-stricken curse foreshadowed that Dasharatha too would die separated from his son, linking to the exile's outcome?
options: A blind ascetic couple whose son Shravana he had killed
questions.explanation: Years earlier Dasharatha had accidentally killed the youth Shravana, whose grieving parents cursed him to die in sorrow over a son.

questions.q: What was the deeper significance of Rama refusing Bharata's plea to return early from exile?
options: He upheld dharma by honoring his father's word even after Dasharatha's death
He preferred forest life to ruling
He feared the people of Ayodhya
questions.explanation: Rama held that his father's word remained binding, completing the full term of exile as the highest duty of dharma.
